name: UpdateItinerary Day-Metadata Round Trip

phase2_single_day.details: UpdateItinerary with 1 day (day_number=1, date=2026-09-01, summary='Arrival in Lisbon', 2 items) wrote and read back correctly. GetItinerary returned summary='Arrival in Lisbon' (not fallback 'Day 1'), date='2026-09-01', and both items with correct titles/descriptions.

phase3_multi_day.details: UpdateItinerary with 3 days all round-tripped correctly. Day 1: summary='Arrival in Lisbon', date='2026-09-01', 2 items. Day 2: summary='Sintra day trip', date='2026-09-02', 3 items. Day 3: summary='Belém and the waterfront', date='2026-09-03', 2 items. Unicode characters (é, ó) preserved correctly.

phase4_summary_only_and_date_only.details: Day 1 with summary='Summary only' and no date: GetItinerary returned summary='Summary only', date omitted (empty string). Day 2 with no summary and date='2026-09-05': GetItinerary returned summary='Day 2' (correct fallback), date='2026-09-05'. Both behaved as expected.

phase5_empty_truncation.details: UpdateItinerary with days:[] cleared all itinerary data. GetItinerary returned itinerary with tripId only and no days array — complete truncation confirmed.

phase6_replacement_overwrites.details: After truncation, UpdateItinerary with fresh 2-day plan (New plan day 1/2, dates 2026-10-15/16, 4 new items). GetItinerary returned only the new 2 days with correct summaries, dates, and items. No stale data from Phase 3's 3-day itinerary.

warnings.description: ItineraryDay.id changes between UpdateItinerary response and subsequent GetItinerary response for the same day. For example, UpdateItinerary returned day id '2c15ad3d-...' but GetItinerary returned '544063d2-...' for the same day_number=1. This suggests day rows are regenerated on read or the day grouping produces new UUIDs. Not a functional bug since items retain stable IDs, but clients should not cache day IDs across calls.

summary: All 6 phases passed. UpdateItinerary correctly preserves day summary and date fields through write-read cycles. Single-day, multi-day, summary-only, date-only, empty truncation, and full replacement all work as expected. The 'Day N' fallback is correctly applied only when no summary is provided. Unicode characters round-trip properly. One informational note: ItineraryDay IDs are non-stable across UpdateItinerary/GetItinerary calls (items IDs are stable).
